item.title: 'Double-edged sword': How Aussie content creators are reacting to the under-16 social media ban
item.description: A﻿s the under-16 social media ban inches closer, questions are being raised about the implications the ban will have on people who target their content toward younger audiences.

item.title: Everything we know about ChatGPT's 'erotica' rollout in December
item.description: Experts from a range of fields, including OpenAI's own wellbeing panel, are worried about what it means for mental health.

item.title: Review: The best game on the Switch 2 yet
item.description: It meets (and arguably exceeds) the exceptionally high bar set by its predecessors and is willing to gamble the franchise's small but loyal fanbase on a few big changes.

item.title: How Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora's From the Ashes expansion aligns with the new film
item.description: Developed in collaboration between Massive Entertainment and Lightstorm Entertainment, the From the Ashes game expansion runs side by side to the events of Avatar: Fire and Ash.

item.title: Apple issues urgent iPhone update after triple zero emergency call testing raises concerns
item.description: Apple has released an emergency update to iOS 26 that applies only to the iPhone 12, addressing a compatibility issue with Australia's emergency calling system.

item.title: The Oscars to abandon broadcast TV, leaving longtime home of US network ABC
item.description: In a seismic shift for one of television’s marquee events, the Academy Awards will depart broadcast TV.

item.title: The TVs of tomorrow: Ultra-thin designs and 130-inch monsters
item.description: CES, the biggest technology show in the world, is happening this week in Las Vegas, a place for the biggest brands and the newest startups to demonstrate their innovation to the world in the hope of making it into retail stores this year.

item.title: 'One Battle After Another' dominates SAG's Actor Awards with seven nominations
item.description: One Battle After Another dominated nominations to the Actor Awards, with Paul Thomas Anderson's ragtag revolutionary saga landing a record seven nods in the annual SAG-AFTRA honours.

item.title: Australian technology companies making it big on the world stage
item.description: A﻿longside the tech giants at the CES trade show in Las Vegas are Australian start-ups displaying their products.

item.title: The biggest video games set to be released this year
item.description: 2026 is going to be see some incredible video games released - from the highly anticipated Grand Theft Auto...

item.title: We got an early look at three of the biggest games coming to Switch 2. This is our verdict
item.description: Here's what you need to know about Mario Tennis Fever, Pragmata and Resident Evil Requiem.

item.title: Seven years on, Fallout 76 developers still listening to fans and now watching the TV series
item.description: Seven years after its launch, video game Fallout 76 continues to evolve, with new updates shaped by both fan feedback and inspiration from Amazon's Fallout TV series.

item.title: Deal reached to save collapsed Healthscope private hospitals
item.description: H﻿ealthscope will become a not-for-profit operator in order to save its suite of hospitals after the company collapsed financially last year.

item.title: Australia quietly moves to impose tariffs on Chinese steel imports
item.description: T﻿he move follows concerns by local businesses they are facing unfair competition, but risks retaliation from Beijing.

item.title: Create your own Pokemon paradise with the latest in Nintendo life-sim
item.description: After an hour rebuilding my own deserted island back from the brink, it's safe to say our favourite pocket monsters remain irresistibly charming.

item.title: ANZ posts nearly $2 billion in quarterly profit, days after charging staff for 'welcome back' sausage sizzle
item.description: ANZ﻿'s cash profit for the first quarter jumped by six per cent to $1.94 billion, partly driven by the thousands of redundancies announced last year.

item.title: Iconic Australian retailer Barbeques Galore collapses into receivership﻿, 500 jobs at risk
item.description: Around 500 jobs are now at risk.

item.title: Australia Post wants to raise stamp prices
item.description: T﻿he national postal service says it's needed to offset losses in its letter division.

item.title: New God of War game headlines 'epic' PlayStation State of Play event
item.description: A new God of War game, exciting trailers, and several major reveals — PlayStation's February State of Play was one of the strongest showcases in recent memory.

item.title: NSW to get an extra public holiday for the next two years after Anzac Day, Premier confirms
item.description: T﻿he decision hasn't been universally popular, but Minns insists the decision is the right thing to do.

item.title: Higher GST, lower income tax, part of plan to lift Australia's economy
item.description: Strong economic medicine is needed over d﻿ebt levels racked up by Australian states, a global body warns.

item.title: Australian food court chain Sushi Sushi sells to Japanese dining empire in estimated $160 million deal
item.description: Sushi Sushi, which began in Melbourne in 1998, has been snapped ﻿up by Tokyo-based Genki Global Dining Concepts.

item.title: Australian gas producer Santos wins court fight over net zero claims
item.description: A lawsuit against gas producer Santos alleged the company misled the public on its plans to achieve net zero carbon emissions.

item.title: Record number of cafes and restaurants going out of business
item.description: T﻿he hospitality industry in Australia is now split between winners and losers, new research shows.

item.title: 'This is peak Australia': NAB chief warns living standards will stagnate without serious reform
item.description: After generations of improvement, our living standards will be stuck in the present – or go backwards – without major economic reform.

item.title: Business busted for making false claims about link to Bondi terror attack
item.description: N﻿SW Fair Trading found the company falsely claimed the proceeds of sales would go to victims and their families.

item.title: Telstra records billion dollar profit, reveals it shed 2356 jobs in 2025
item.description: T﻿he job cuts have saved $118 million alone.

item.title: eBay to buy Depop in $1.7b move to reach Gen Z and Millennials
item.description: e﻿Bay hopes buying Depop will help it reach a younger and more fashion-forward audience.

item.title: How a blood test could predict if and when you could get Alzheimer's
item.description: A﻿lzheimer's is the biggest cause of dementia, which is the leading cause of death in Australia.

item.title: Australians aren't eating enough burritos. It just cost one company hundreds of millions of dollars
item.description: I﻿ts share price has dropped by more than half in the last 12 months.

item.title: Employees at Aussie start-up Eucalyptus set to receive windfall after $1.6 billion acquisition
item.description: Employee shareholders at an Aussie start-up are set for a big pay day after the company was acquired for $1.6 billion.

item.title: WiseTech Global set to cut up to 2000 jobs for 'AI efficiency'
item.description: T﻿he software company said ongoing developments in AI would deliver a leaner organisation.

item.title: Domino's Pizza sheds 10 per cent of customers after reducing discounts
item.description: Domino's started offering fewer discounts, and some of its customers weren't too happy.

item.title: Man fired after pouring cooking oil down the drain outside popular bakery
item.description: The incident quickly gained attention, with members of the community saying the man's actions were "pure laziness", "disgusting", and ﻿"such a shame".

item.title: Samsung's new phone feature blocks prying eyes
item.description: Samsung's flagship smartphone for 2026 will launch with an industry-first privacy feature aimed at stopping those prying eyes around you from glimpsing what you're looking at on your screen.

item.title: Glue Store to close in shake-up by retail giant
item.description: The company said it now wants to focus on its global brands.

item.title: Qantas unveils major change to frequent flyer program as airline records bumper $1.46 billion profit
item.description: Qantas has announced major changes to its frequent flyer program after posting a bumper $1.46 billion half-year profit.

item.title: Company behind Australian cultural icon Hills Hoist could soon be sold
item.description: The company behind one of Australia's most iconic brands is set to be put up for sale as its American owner focuses on operations closer to home.

item.title: Almost 40,000 Budget Direct customers allegedly overcharged $3.3 million
item.description: Australia's financial watchdog has taken legal action against the insurer behind the Budget Direct products.

item.title: VB pins hopes on mid-strength as Aussies drop classic beers
item.description: T﻿he newly-unveiled mid-strength brew signals a growing trend across Australia as illustrated by the sales numbers.

item.title: Dire shortage of healthcare providers capable of performing potentially life-changing endometriosis scans
item.description: A life-changing ultrasound saved her from that fate, but there's a dire shortage of people capable of performing them in Australia.

item.title: Australia's gender pay gap is creeping down, but some major Australian employers are lagging
item.description: Many major Australian brands are failing to turn the dial on the gender pay gap, with some even slipping backwards in the past year, a detailed new report has revealed.

item.title: Aussie pubs, jobs safe for now as UK company lays off hundreds of staff
item.description: The company has four breweries and about 100 pubs around the globe, including five here in Australia.

item.title: Apple unveils $899 MacBook Neo in surprise launch
item.description: It's a brand new model for the tech giant, and it's a big shift away from the typical price point.

item.title: Australia's most trusted and distrusted brands revealed as local favourites dominate
item.description: S﻿ome brands soared and others struggled.

item.title: Business owner sends premier $24,000 bill after new public holiday announced
item.description: "I'm not arguing against a public holiday, but we were given four months' notice, so we never budgeted for this in our costings."

item.title: Australian markets suffer $90 billion wipeout as experts issue stark warning
item.description: At one stage today, the ASX had sunk by as much as 4.3 per cent, which would have put it on track for the steepest single-day drop since the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020.

item.title: Apple's latest iPhone is designed for those clinging onto older phones
item.description: A lot of people using older model phones are contemplating an upgrade.

item.title: Bigfoot, trombone, orca among the latest emojis
item.description: Have you ever wanted to send a text about orcas or Bigfoot or trombones only to find you have to do it using words instead of pictures?

item.description: That's AUD, not USD. How does the laptop market survive in its current form when the MacBook Neo exists?

item.title: Lululemon fined more than $700,000 over marketing emails
item.description: A﻿CMA found more than 370,000 emails the company sent were in breach of Australian law.

item.title: South Australia Liberal candidate under fire for controversial comments labelling feminism, homosexuality 'dem
item.description: ﻿A Liberal candidate in South Australia's state election has come under fire for controversial comments calling feminism and homosexuality "demonic".

item.title: The government granted Iranian footballers asylum. Then, it moved to block 7000 Iranians from Australia
item.description: T﻿he government introduced proposed changes just hours after granting Iranian footballers asylum in Australia.

item.description: "Where is the fuel gone? Is it the cartels just hoarding it?"

item.title: Aussie software giant Atlassian cuts 1600 jobs in push to AI
item.description: The CEO, one of Australia's richest men, said employees would get an email "within 20 minutes" to find out if they had lost their jobs.

item.title: Australia's No.1 retailer named as last year's winner plunges
item.description: T﻿he new king of brick-and-mortar retailers has been decided.

item.title: Twist for man accused of threatening to kidnap Prime Minister Anthony Albanese
item.description: An allegation that a man threatened serious harm against the prime minister in an online post has been dropped by prosecutors.

item.title: 'Un-Australian': Energy Minister Chris Bowen warns against re-selling petrol on Facebook Marketplace for profi
item.description: Drivers in Australia have been seen stockpiling fuel as petrol and diesel prices soar in response to the conflict in the Middle East.﻿

item.title: Man in intensive care after brawl at Melbourne pizza shop
item.description: A Melbourne man is in intensive care after a brawl broke out at a pizza shop in front of shocked diners.

item.description: T﻿he cost of the visa doubled overnight this month.

item.title: At least 23,000 jobs in tech have been lost to AI this year. Experts say worse is still to come
item.description: T﻿ech company bosses have had similar things to say about AI.

item.title: Trump official issues threat to broadcasters over coverage of Middle East war
item.description: "Broadcasters that are running hoaxes and news distortions - also known as the fake news - have a chance now to correct course."

item.title: Three more footballers reverse asylum decision, Iranian media mocks so-called 'disgraceful failure'
item.description: The team were branded "traitors" by the Islamic regime when they refused to sing the Iranian national anthem before a game in the Asia Cup.

item.title: New laws planned to crack down on Victoria's tobacco wars
item.description: The Victorian government is planning to introduce new laws to try and extinguish Melbourne's tobacco war, which include powers to forcibly shut down dodgy retailers.

item.title: One Nation surges in the polls, stripping potential votes from Labor and the Coalition
item.description: The federal opposition fell to a record low of 22 per cent.

item.description: As the firebrand Queenslander strips voters from Coalition and Labor, we take a deep dive into her politics.

item.description: P﻿rosecutors argued that the employee was merely acting in the company's "Open Company, No Bullshit" philosophy.

item.description: It comes as major fuel companies have been hauled in front of the consumer watchdog for a please-explain.

item.description: "There are hundreds of thousands of people who don't use their firearms for the reason on their licence."

item.description: The owner said it was a "sad and difficult" decision to appoint administrators. ﻿

item.description: The federal government has dropped its two clearest hints to date it is preparing to wind back the capital gains tax (CGT) discount for property investors in the upcoming budget.

item.description: "The Iranian regime are engaging in frankly random attacks across the Gulf region."

item.description: T﻿he shock jock said ARN "decided to try and burn the place down" over an on-air spat with Jackie O.

item.description: The prime minister will talk to state premiers and chief ministers today in a national cabinet meeting aimed at ensuring Australia has a united response to the global oil supply shock.

item.description: T﻿he concerning data comes as Jacinta Allan insists speculation about a leadership coup is "anonymous speculation".

item.description: One Nation has decimated the Liberals and claimed its first lower house seat won at an election outside of Queensland.

item.description: It comes after Trump threatened to send in immigration officers amid a funding shutdown.

item.description: Motorists are being encouraged to use a cheaper alternative at the bowser amid concern about fuel prices﻿ skyrocketing.

item.description: Radvinsky transformed the British-based company into an online pornography powerhouse.

item.description: E﻿ight years after negotiations started, Canberra has signed a free-trade deal with the European Union.

item.title: Man charged for allegedly smashing NSW Parliament House window with barbell
item.description: A man has been arrested after a NSW Parliament House window was smashed with a metal barbell early ﻿this morning.

item.title: Why we can continue to call our cheese feta, but fontina will be a no-go
item.description: T﻿he names used in dozens of common goods will change under new rules in the recently inked Australia-European Union trade agreement.

item.title: Aussies heading to the USA set for high-speed Wi-Fi in a travel revolution
item.description: Australians heading to the United States are set for a big change in the way we travel, with an all-new in-flight experience on the next-generation of aircraft from this airline.

item.title: All households to suffer if 'central' industry buckles under soaring fuel costs
item.description: The global fuel crisis has left one of the nation's most essential sectors heavily exposed.

item.title: Mining giant handed $2 billion in taxpayer funds to keep Australia's second-largest aluminium smelter running
item.description: Rio Tinto has secured a $2 billion taxpayer handout to keep Australia's second-largest aluminium smelter running in a bid to save 3000 jobs.

item.title: Sydney Opera House sails light up to mark 50 Years of Apple
item.description: The sails of the Sydney Opera House will light up with colour over the next three nights as part of a global celebration of Apple's 50th birthday.

item.title: Experts liken sky-high fuel prices to three interest rate rises
item.description: Experts are warning the economic shock from sky-high fuel prices is the equivalent of three interest rate rises.

item.title: Yemen's Houthis may cut off trade route between Asia and Europe, Iran claims
item.description: A powerful militia could cut off one of the world's most important shipping routes in solidarity with Iran.

item.title: 'Iran's enemies' preparing ground invasion of island, says top politician
item.description: The US is unlikely to launch a ground invasion of any of Iran's islands, an Australian expert on the Strait of Hormuz has said.

item.title: Fines for dodgy petrol pricing doubled as fuel crisis continues
item.description: T﻿he government is trying to respond to growing anxiety about the fuel crisis sparked by the war in the Middle East.

item.description: US President Donald Trump issued a warning to Tehran on social media to "get serious soon" on negotiating a deal to end the war.

item.title: 'Almost everything has gone up': Payday super laws are coming at a fraught time for small business owners
item.description: Rebecca Foley, who co-owns Sprout Artisan Bakery in Brisbane, said ﻿unpredictable costs and new compliances have been creeping into her profit margin for years.

item.title: Today in History - March 27: Illness began with headache, then left musician with a 20-second memory
item.description: Renowned UK classical musician Clive Wearing contracted a disease that gave him perhaps the most severe form of amnesia ever recorded on March 27, 1985.

item.description: The top-rating provocateur is suing his former employer ARN in the Federal Court.

item.description: Trump's right-hand man has raised the spectre of a device capable of killing "tens of thousands of people".

item.description: Donald Trump's signature will be added to all US banknotes in a first for a sitting president.

item.title: White House posting heavily pixelated images on social media
item.description: The White House's official social media accounts have been posting heavily pixelated images of administration officials, with no explanation why.

item.title: Israel warns attacks on Iran 'will escalate and expand' as Trump claims advances in ceasefire talks
item.description: Israel threatened to "escalate and expand" its attacks on Iran, even as US President Donald Trump claimed talks on ending the war were going well.

item.title: Today in History - March 28: He didn't speak to anyone for 15 months, with no idea he had become Japan's most
item.description: A Japanese comedian who spent 15 months living in complete isolation found out he was unwittingly a reality TV star on March 28, 1999.

item.description: Iran has vowed revenge after Israel struck two nuclear sites in the country overnight.

item.description: In the attack, the man drunkenly beat up the victim, when he became jealous of her friendship with Barron Trump.

item.description: It was all thanks to a little nudge from her mother on an international family trip when she was eight. ﻿

item.description: In an act of punctuation compromise that would have dramatic geopolitical ramifications, the new parliament in Czechoslovakia settled on an official name on March 29, 1990.

item.description: The figures don't paint an overly pretty picture for Australia – although the government says there's a good reason for it.

item.description: Sweeping changes to Victorian rental laws will kick in next week, making it easier - and a little cheaper - for prospective tenants.

item.description: T﻿he bust comes as the AFP pours resoruces into disrupting the South Pacific drug trade.

item.title: Israeli airstrike in southern Lebanon kills three journalists covering the war
item.description: Top officials in Lebanon condemned the strike, with President Joseph Aoun calling it a “flagrant crime that violates all laws and agreements that protect journalists.”

item.description: French police have thwarted a suspected bomb attack outside a Bank of America building in Paris, authorities said.

item.description: T﻿he move is a bid to shore up fuel supply and bring down the cost of petrol.

item.title: Dezi Freeman's final moments and the dramatic police operation that brought him down
item.description: Seven months after he killed two police officers, Dezi Freeman emerged from a shipping container draped in a blanket. Soon after, Australia's most wanted fugitive was dead.

item.description: Mecca's parent company and associated businesses have each coughed up ﻿$198,000 in fines after being accused of late lodgements by the corporate watchdog.

item.description: In the past few weeks, just about every positive swing on the markets has stemmed from Donald Trump flagging a possible end to his war with Iran.

item.description: Steve Wozniak, Steve Jobs and Ronald Wayne founded Apple Computers on April 1, 1976.

item.title: Iran attacks Pete Hegseth on reports he sought to profit from the war
item.description: The Pentagon has vociferously denied reports that Secretary of War Pete Hegseth sought to make multi-million dollar investments in defence stocks in the weeks before the Iran war began.

item.title: Major airport in Florida to be renamed for Donald Trump
item.description: When Donald Trump lands in Florida for his almost weekly trips to his country club Mar-a-Lago, he'll be seeing his name even more than usual.

item.title: Pete Hegseth lifts suspension on Kid Rock helicopter crews
item.description: US Secretary of Defence Pete Hegseth has lifted the suspensions of helicopter crews who flew close to the home of singer Kid Rock in an apparent stunt.

item.title: 'Construction has to stop!': Judge orders a halt to Trump's ballroom
item.description: A judge has halted Donald Trump's construction of a ballroom at the White House.

item.description: The store will continue trading until October 2026.

item.title: Prosecutor could call hundreds of witnesses to accused killer cop's trial
item.description: Prosecutors could call hundreds of witnesses to give evidence into ex-police officer Beaumont Lamarre-Condon's double murder trial.

item.description: Everything you need to know.

item.title: SA's top cop 'absolutely certain' police will return to home of missing toddler Gus Lamont as he hopes for 'br
item.description: The four-year-old was last seen playing on a dirt mound at his family's property Oak Park Station in South Australia around 5pm on September 27.

item.title: Man pleads guilty to teacher's murder after remains found in buried freezer
item.description: Bobby Andrew Weaver pleaded guilty to murder seven years after body parts were found in a chest freezer buried in the victim's backyard.

item.title: Fugitive Dezi Freeman fired stolen gun twice before death in a hail of police bullets
item.description: The stolen gun wielded by the double police killer was likely shot out of his hands, John Silvester says.

item.title: Wyndham city mayor Preet Singh temporarily stands down after providing character references for a sex offender
item.description: The shock exit follows mounting pressure from the community to resign.

item.title: Phones, smartwatches and earbuds to be banned in every Victorian school after government extends device crackd
item.description: Victoria's nation-first phone ban was first introduced to government schools in 2020 and will now capture all schools, including private and Catholic, from January 28, 2027.

item.title: AS IT HAPPENED: PM urges against panic buying
item.description: It comes as some petrol stations in a capital city fast-tracked significant savings for Aussies at the bowser.

item.title: Three-month fuel excise cost passes Senate in bid to fight high prices
item.description: Fuel price relief has been made official but drivers are warned it will take time for the excise cut to be felt in their wallets.

item.title: As fuel excise set to be halved, when will fuel prices in Australia go down?
item.description: With key taxes being slashed, when will Australians finally see relief at the pump?

item.title: Tiger Woods pleads not guilty to DUI in car crash where authorities say he was found with opioids
item.description: The golfer's eyes were bloodshot and glassy, his pupils dilated and he had hydrocodone pills in his pocket when interviewed at the scene of his car crash.

item.title: All the roads where double demerits don't apply this Easter long weekend
item.description: F﻿our states and territories simply don't enforce double demerits.

item.title: Teen shot by police after allegedly pulling out knife during welfare check
item.description: Officers were called to a home on Akebia Street﻿ in Arana Hills earlier today to conduct a welfare check.

item.title: Grandfather found guilty after claiming he unknowingly smuggled 2.5kg of heroin into Sydney Airport
item.description: The 70-year-old was carrying a green bag containing a camp bed with a mosquito net, which had 48 packages of heroin hidden within the metal frame.

item.title: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese warns 'economic shocks' to be 'with us for months' in rare national address
item.description: The PM insisted the government was doing everything it could but his rare message has been slammed by opponents as a "nothing burger".

item.title: Western Australia enacts emergency fuel powers as PM urges calm
item.description: The state's six fuel suppliers will have 48 hours to hand over "sensitive information", or risk being fined up to $100,000 per day.

item.title: Passengers stranded in moving traffic after robotaxi outage in China's Wuhan
item.description: Some robotaxi passengers were left stranded in the middle of fast-moving traffic in a major Chinese city after their driverless vehicles stopped running.

item.title: Transatlantic rift widens as Trump lashes out at NATO allies over unpopular Mideast war
item.description: The US President said he is strongly considering pulling the US out of NATO, ratcheting up his criticism of European allies and exposing a wider rift in the transatlantic alliance.

item.title: 'Not all that reassuring': Albanese faces the nation with call for calm but little detail
item.description: After hours of build-up and speculation, Prime Minister Anthony Albanese's rare prime-time address to the nation landed with more of a shrug than a shockwave.

item.title: Today in History - April 2: A few milligrams from lab leak killed dozens of people
item.description: A northeasterly wind scattered a few milligrams of anthrax spores from a military facility in the Soviet city of Sverdlovsk on April 2, 1979.

item.title: 'Get your own oil': Trump is refusing to deal with the Strait of Hormuz. But he might be bluffing
item.description: Trump's suggestion that the US will not assist in reopening the Strait of Hormuz to ships might a slight exaggeration.

item.title: Easter trading hours 2026: When shops are open this long weekend
item.description: While most stores will be shut on Friday, there are some exceptions and varying trading hours until Monday.﻿

item.title: Prosecutors assisting police in ex-prince Andrew investigation
item.description: T﻿he former royal is being investigated over his links to sex offender and financier Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: 'High' chance of new cyclone in coming days
item.description: M﻿ultiple factors could see a tropical low develop into a severe cyclone by the middle of next week.

item.title: DNA confirms notorious killer carried out unsolved 1974 murder
item.description: Teen Laura Aime's death was unsolved for over 50 years, until new forensic technology led to a breakthrough.

item.title: Oracle slashes 30,000 staff in brutal 6am email
item.description: Oracle has laid off nearly 20 per cent of its workforce to free up billions of dollars in cash for AI.

item.title: Aged care home to pay $11.7 million after 5,500 staff underpaid
item.description: In one of the largest wage remediation cases to hit the Australian aged care sector, Southern Cross Care will pay back $11.7 million to thousands of its workers.

item.title: Iranian leader's letter to US citizens asks if war is 'America First'
item.description: P﻿resident Masoud Pezeshkian posted the letter to the people of the US online to ask if their interests were served.

item.title: Red meat shortage looms after majority of Australian producers warn current fuel supply will dry up within one
item.description: A survey released by the Red Meat ­Advisory Council (RMAC) found that 85 per cent of red meat producers only have enough fuel to keep operations going for one more week.

item.title: Donald Trump storms out of Supreme Court as he faces loss
item.description: Donald Trump has stormed out of the Supreme Court as one of his keystone policies came under the scrutiny of a sceptical bench.

item.title: Trump vows to bomb Iran 'back to the Stone Age' in national address
item.description: S﻿oon after his speech began, Wall Street futures went into a nosedive.

item.title: PM defends lacklustre national address
item.description: The speech is widely rumoured to involve financial assistance for businesses hit by soaring fuel costs.

item.title: One person dead, homes damaged following 7.4-magnitude earthquake
item.description: The 7.4-magnitude quake struck beneath the Molucca Sea at a depth of about 35 kilometres.

item.title: What our readers thought of Prime Minister Anthony Albanese's address to the nation
item.description: "A waste of time" – those were the words nine.com.au readers used over and over again.

item.title: Aerial images provide the closest look yet at Dezi Freeman's hideout
item.description: Police last night left the Thologolong property where the double police killer died in a volley of gunfire on Monday.

item.title: Neo-Nazi forced offline after alleged threats to MPs
item.description: Joel Davis, 31, has been in custody on remand since his arrest in November for allegedly calling for the "rhetorical rape" of independent Wentworth MP Allegra Spender.

item.title: Mosquito warning ahead of Easter holidays
item.description: Recent monitoring of mosquitoes has found Ross River virus activity in mosquito trappings in parts of Greater Western Sydney.

item.title: We were all set for prime time, then we were let down with a thud by our leaders
item.description: Opinion: In terms of doom and gloom, we're being bombarded at the moment with bad news.

item.title: Thunderstorms, showers and sun: States and territories in for a mixed bag this Easter long weekend
item.description: Showers will lurk across the weekend in many states.

item.title: Experts say there are 'strong signs' El Niño could hit Australia later this year, bringing hot temperatures an
item.description: T﻿here are strong indications that hotter-than-usual temperatures could hit Australia later this year.

item.title: Australia to suffer 'sharp recession' under prolonged war in Iran, economist report says
item.description: The opportunity to get out of the war in Iran ﻿is "narrowing". A longer conflict looks more likely.

item.title: Where you can and can't buy alcohol over the Easter long weekend
item.description: Good Friday and the Easter weekend are some of the only days of the year that have specific restrictions on alcohol sales.

item.title: Shoppers scramble as youths brawl at Melbourne retail plaza
item.description: Vision shows a stampede of more than 50 youths surrounding a group of females who were throwing punches and kicks at other girls on the ground.

item.title: 'We must look after ourselves': PM hit with three key demands
item.description: Angus Taylor has taken aim at Anthony Albanese following his national address last night.

item.title: Man killed in western Sydney stabbing
item.description: Videos posted to social media showed more than a dozen police cars, ambulances and the police helicopter responding to the incident.﻿

item.title: NASA successfully launches four astronauts to the moon on humanity's first lunar voyage in 53 years
item.description: History has been made today with NASA's rocket blasting off to the moon with four astronauts on board.

item.title: Aussies may have to ration fuel as 'last resort', expert says
item.description: I﻿t comes as ﻿Prime Minister Anthony Albanese encouraged people to avoid driving where they can.

item.title: 'Important step': Marles defends PM's no-news national address
item.description: T﻿he deputy prime minister said Albanese had made the right decision to speak to the people of Australia.

item.title: Major gambling reforms to cap betting TV ads between 6am and 8.30pm
item.description: "We don't want kids growing up thinking that footy and gambling are inextricably linked."

item.title: Bid to suppress names of alleged Bondi shooter denied in court
item.description: A move to hide the names of alleged Bondi terrorist Naveed Akram's family has been rejected despite death threats and harassment by vigilantes.

item.title: Entertainment news headlines - 9News
item.description: Breaking entertainment news including celebrity gossip, film and movie news, music news, award ceremonies, scandals, photos and videos

item.title: Latest technology news headlines, updates & new tech innovation | 9News
item.description: Latest technology news stories including consumer electronics, science and space technology, gadgets, internet tech, medical devices, websites and social tech, cryptocurrencies and other technology news headlines

item.title: Latest health news top stories & analysis
item.description: Latest health news, research and results, scientific discoveries, diets, obesity, cancer, medical procedures and medicine, hospitals, and government health guidelines

item.title: Business news headlines - 9News
item.description: Latest business news, company tax, government regulations, CEOs, entrepreneurs, customers, and big business strategy

item.title: Politics News - 9News - Latest political news today Australia and World
item.description: Latest political news from Australia and around the world, covering elections, by-elections, our regular leadership spills, poll results, Question Time debates and daily Australian party politics

item.title: World news headlines and international current affairs - 9News
item.description: Latest world news headlines: International breaking news and current affairs from US, Europe, Middle East, Asia, Africa and more. If it happens around the world, you will know about it on 9News

item.title: News Australia - Latest news headlines in Australia
item.description: Today's news in major cities, regional and local areas which can include accident reports, police & emergency responses, criminal and court proceedings or live breaking news
